bugs.error_message: Shapes (rect, circle, arrow) don't start where the mouse is clicked; drawing from top of screenshot starts below the actual click point
bugs.root_cause: Canvas used `object-fit: contain` CSS which letterboxes the bitmap inside the element. getBoundingClientRect() returns the full element bounds including the empty letterbox areas, so toCanvasPx() mapped coordinates against the wrong origin and scale — producing a systematic offset proportional to the letterbox size.
bugs.fix: Removed `object-contain` from canvas element. Added ResizeObserver on wrapper div to track available space reactively. Added bitmapW/bitmapH reactive refs to useAnnotationCanvas composable (updated in initCanvas). Component computes CSS display size as Math.min(wrapperW/bitmapW, wrapperH/bitmapH) * bitmap dimensions — canvas element exactly matches bitmap aspect ratio, so getBoundingClientRect() is always correct and toCanvasPx() requires no offset adjustment.

bugs.error_message: Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'get')
bugs.root_cause: chrome.storage.local is unavailable in content script contexts inside iframes (e.g. Gmail). Bare calls to chrome.storage.local.get/set/remove throw TypeError when storage is inaccessible, crashing the content script on startup.
bugs.fix: Wrapped all chrome.storage.local calls in try/catch. getTheme() falls back to 'light', getAuth() returns null, set/remove/toggle calls silently swallow the error.

bugs.root_cause: Async call without await — returned Promise instead of value

bugs.error_message: Clicking 'Create issue' closes the modal but doesn't open the new issue tab
bugs.root_cause: openInTab() called chrome.tabs?.create() from the content script. chrome.tabs is undefined in content scripts, so the optional chain silently no-ops (no throw → window.open fallback never fires). emit('close') still ran, tearing down the modal with nothing else happening.
bugs.fix: Added OPEN_TAB message handler in background.ts that calls chrome.tabs.create. ScreenshotCapture.openInTab now sends chrome.runtime.sendMessage({ type: 'OPEN_TAB', url }), with window.open fallback if the send rejects.

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'get') — chrome.storage.local.get() throws in Gmail iframe context
bugs.root_cause: chrome.storage API is inaccessible inside cross-origin iframes even when the `storage` permission is declared. Sites like Gmail embed many iframes; the content script injected into those iframes throws when calling chrome.storage methods.
bugs.fix: Wrapped every chrome.storage call in try/catch. getTheme() returns 'light', getAuth() returns null, set/clear ops no-op silently on failure. watchTheme/onThemeChange return () => {} on failure.

bugs.error_message: Placeholder text invisible in IssueComposePanel — title and description placeholders not visible in light or dark mode
bugs.root_cause: Used `placeholder:text-muted` which maps to --muted (93% lightness warm gray). The token resolves to an almost-invisible color on light backgrounds.
bugs.fix: Changed to `placeholder:text-muted-foreground/60` — visible but clearly distinct from real input text.

bugs.error_message: Browser default 3D button borders (outset ButtonFace) showing through shadcn buttons inside shadow DOM
bugs.root_cause: Without `@tailwind base` (preflight), browser applies `button { border: 2px outset ButtonFace; appearance: auto }` which leaks through shadow DOM.
bugs.fix: Added `@tailwind base` to content.css so preflight runs inside the shadow root. WXT rewrites :root to :host automatically.

bugs.error_message: shadcn Select dropdown escapes shadow DOM — loses all CSS tokens, renders unstyled on host page
bugs.root_cause: reka-ui's SelectPortal teleports to document.body by default; CSS tokens are scoped to shadow root so content teleported outside loses all styling.
bugs.fix: Added `to?: string | Element` prop to SelectContent, forwarded to SelectPortal. Extension passes shadow root; web app uses default 'body'.

bugs.error_message: Minified Vue error chain (hP/Jr/q2/zt) thrown when clicking Pause or Stop in recording control bar; click handlers did nothing.
bugs.root_cause: defineEmits<{...}>() was called without assigning the return to a const emit variable. The template referenced emit('toggle-pause') and emit('stop') — emit was undefined, so every click threw a ReferenceError that Vue surfaced through its async error handler.
bugs.fix: Use const emit = defineEmits<{...}>(). Always assign defineEmits to a variable when the template uses emit(...).

bugs.error_message: Toolbar disappears on captured-tab reload; popup Stop button broken after offscreen refactor
bugs.root_cause: content.ts still owned the inline MediaRecorder lifecycle (startRecording, acquireTabStream, togglePause, stopRecording, recorder.onstop). Reload destroyed the JS context that held the recorder, losing the toolbar and the stop button target.
bugs.fix: Stripped all inline recording code. Added mountControlBar(startedAtEpoch) that mounts toolbar and sends RECORDING_COMMAND to background. Added handleRecordingFinalised() that decodes base64 blob and opens review modal. Added reload recovery via GET_RECORDING_STATE on init. Popup onRecord() sends START_RECORDING_FLOW; onStopRecording() sends RECORDING_COMMAND stop — both direct to background.

bugs.error_message: Timeline event timestamps wrong after page reload mid-recording (performance.now() resets to 0 on new page)
bugs.root_cause: startCaptureStreams used performance.now() - startedAt. After a page reload performance.now() resets, so timestamps from the new page start near 0.
bugs.fix: Changed param to startedAtEpoch (Date.now() value from background), tsMs() now uses Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtEpoch).

bugs.error_message: Popup elapsed timer shows NaN — useRecordingStatus reads startedAt but background writes startedAtEpoch
bugs.root_cause: RecordingState interface had startedAt: number but background.ts writes { tabId, pageUrl, startedAtEpoch }. Property name mismatch caused Date.now() - undefined = NaN.
bugs.fix: Updated RecordingState to { startedAtEpoch, pageUrl, tabId } to match background storage format. Updated applyState and tick to use startedAtEpoch.
